choices.text: Ask who was around
Ask about the time window
Ask about prior contact
Press the contradiction
Pin down the exact location
Frame it as the victim bringing trouble
Frame it as pressure and circumstance
Frame it as someone trying to protect something
Frame it as something that got out of hand

nodes.text: There was movement near {place} {time_phrase}. If you want the name that keeps sticking in my head, it is {suspect}.

choices.text: Ask whether anyone else was there
Ask what made the figure stand out

nodes.text: Not close enough to swear there was company, but the air around {place} felt crowded, like the night had more than one set of shoes in it.
The thing I remember is the pause. Whoever it was stopped near {place} like they were listening for the room to answer back.
It was {time_phrase}. I remember because the whole block had that after-hours hush right before something breaks it.

choices.text: Ask what happened just before
Ask what happened just after

nodes.text: Before that, nothing but city noise and my own nerves. Then everything around {place} seemed to hold its breath at once.
After that, the sound dropped away too fast. That is the part that stayed with me. Trouble usually leaves an echo. This one packed itself up.
I knew the shape of {suspect} before I knew the name. People drift around {place}; some of them start to feel like weather.

choices.text: Ask whether they ever spoke
Ask whether the witness trusted them

nodes.text: Nothing worth calling a conversation. A nod here, a glance there. The kind of contact that tells you enough to keep your wallet close.
Trust is a fancy word for this city. I knew enough not to turn my back too long, and not enough to call that proof of anything.
You want it to line up neat because paper likes straight lines. Nights like this do not. They smear.

choices.text: Tell them the times do not match
Tell them they are withholding the useful part

nodes.text: Fine. Maybe I slid the time to keep my own name out of it. {time_phrase} is the version I can still live with.
Useful to who? You, maybe. To me it is just another reason not to get remembered by the wrong people around {place}.
You keep saying {place} like it is one clean point on a map. It is not. It is doorways, shadow lines, rain gutters, bad angles.

choices.text: Ask for the doorway or side of the building
Ask what the witness could actually see

nodes.text: Closer to the side where people think nobody is looking. That is as exact as I can give you without pretending the dark was kinder than it was.
Enough to know somebody was there. Not enough to make a saint swear to it in daylight. That is the truth of it.
You can dress it up however you like, but trouble was already leaning on the block before the blood showed. {place} had that kind of gravity.

choices.text: Ask whether the victim invited the danger
Ask whether the witness is shifting blame

nodes.text: I am saying people make patterns and then act surprised when the pattern closes around them. That is not justice. It is just the street being itself.
Maybe I am. Maybe everybody in this city shifts blame the way they shift collars in the rain. It still does not make the air around {place} feel innocent.
Circumstance does half the killing in this town. Bad timing, thin money, the wrong door left open, the wrong soul already frayed.

choices.text: Ask what pressure was building
Ask whether the witness is excusing it

nodes.text: The kind you do not photograph. Bills. Humiliation. People owing things they cannot pay back in cash. The room was already loaded before anyone touched it.
No. I am saying ugly choices are easier to make when the whole week has been leaning on your throat.
Sometimes people tell themselves they are protecting somebody. That is how they walk into a ruin and call it duty.

choices.text: Ask who was supposed to be protected
Ask whether that story sounds convenient

nodes.text: Could be themselves. Could be somebody upstairs, asleep, owed, or too naive to know the tab was coming due. People say 'for them' when they mean 'so I can stand myself after.'
Of course it is convenient. Mercy is one of the prettier lies because it lets everyone keep their face.
You want accidental because it lets the world stay crooked instead of rotten. I get that. But accidents still leave a hand on them.

choices.text: Ask whether it looked sudden
Ask whether someone tried to fix it after

nodes.text: Sudden, maybe. Clean, no. Even panic has a rhythm once it starts stepping on the room.
That is the part I cannot shake. However it started, somebody stayed long enough to think about what came next.
